heading: Use of average sales price payment methodology
label: 1395w–3a Use of average sales price payment methodology
notes: References in TextSubsection (g)(7), referred to in subsec. (i)(4)(C), does not define “applicable period” because subsec. (g) of this section does not have a par. (7). However, such term is defined in .section 1395w–114b(g)(7) of this title

Effective Date of 2010 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–148, title III, § 3139(b)124 Stat. 440Mar. 23, 2010“The amendments made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply to payments for biosimilar biological products beginning with the first day of the second calendar quarter after enactment of legislation providing for a biosimilar pathway (as determined by the Secretary [probably means the Secretary of Health and Human Services]).”

Report on Sales to Pharmacy Benefit Managers, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–173, title III, § 303(c)(2)117 Stat. 2245Dec. 8, 2003“(A).—StudyThe Secretary [of Health and Human Services] shall conduct a study on sales of drugs and biologicals to large volume purchasers, such as pharmacy benefit managers and health maintenance organizations, for purposes of determining whether the price at which such drugs and biologicals are sold to such purchasers does not represent the price such drugs and biologicals are made available for purchase to prudent physicians.“(B).—ReportNot later than , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the study conducted under paragraph (1), and shall include recommendations on whether such sales to large volume purchasers should be excluded from the computation of a manufacturer’s average sales price under section 1847A of the Social Security Act [], as added by paragraph (1).”January 1, 200642 U.S.C. 1395w–3a

Inspector General Report on Adequacy of Reimbursement Rate Under Average Sales Price Methodology, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–173, title III, § 303(c)(3)117 Stat. 2245Dec. 8, 2003“(A).—StudyThe Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services shall conduct a study on the ability of physician practices in the specialties of hematology, hematology/oncology, and medical oncology of different sizes, especially particularly large practices, to obtain drugs and biologicals for the treatment of cancer patients at 106 percent of the average sales price for the drugs and biologicals. In conducting the study, the Inspector General shall conduct an audit of a representative sample of such practices to determine the adequacy of reimbursement under section 1847A of the Social Security Act [], as added by paragraph (1).42 U.S.C. 1395w–3a“(B).—ReportNot later , the Inspector General shall submit to Congress a report on the study conducted under subparagraph (A), and shall include recommendations on the adequacy of reimbursement for such drugs and biologicals under such section 1847A [].”October 1, 200542 U.S.C. 1395w–3a

L. 108–173section 303 of Pub. L. 108–173section 304 of Pub. L. 108–173section 1395u of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), this section shall apply to payment for drugs and biologicals that are described in section 1395u()(1)(C) of this title and that are furnished on or after .oJanuary 1, 2005

children.heading: Election
children.label: 2 Election
children.text: This section shall not apply in the case of a physician who elects under subsection (a)(1)(A)(ii) of for that section to apply instead of this section for the payment for drugs and biologicals.section 1395w–3b of this title

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a multiple source drug (as defined in subsection (c)(6)(C)), 106 percent of the amount determined under paragraph (3) for a multiple source drug furnished before , or 106 percent of the amount determined under paragraph (6) for a multiple source drug furnished on or after ;April 1, 2008April 1, 2008

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a single source drug or biological (as defined in subsection (c)(6)(D)), 106 percent of the amount determined under paragraph (4) or in the case of such a drug or biological product that is a selected drug (as referred to in ), with respect to a price applicability period (as defined in ), 106 percent of the maximum fair price (as defined in ) applicable for such drug and a year during such period; orsection 1320f–1(c) of this titlesection 1320f(b)(2) of this titlesection 1320f(c)(3) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of a biosimilar biological product (as defined in subsection (c)(6)(H)), the amount determined under paragraph (8).

children.heading: Specification by manufacturer
children.label: A Specification by manufacturer
children.text: The manufacturer of a drug or biological shall specify the unit associated with each National Drug Code (including package size) as part of the submission of data under or subsection (f)(2), as applicable.section 1396r–8(b)(3)(A)(iii) of this title

children.heading: Unit defined
children.label: B Unit defined
children.text: In this section, the term “unit” means, with respect to each National Drug Code (including package size) associated with a drug or biological, the lowest identifiable quantity (such as a capsule or tablet, milligram of molecules, or grams) of the drug or biological that is dispensed, exclusive of any diluent without reference to volume measures pertaining to liquids. For years after 2004, the Secretary may establish the unit for a manufacturer to report and methods for counting units as the Secretary determines appropriate to implement this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the manufacturer’s average sales price (as defined in subsection (c)); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the total number of units specified under paragraph (2) sold; and

children.label: B
children.text: dividing the sum determined under subparagraph (A) by the sum of the total number of units under subparagraph (A)(ii) for all National Drug Codes assigned to such drug products.

children.heading: Average sales price
children.label: A Average sales price
children.text: The average sales price as determined using the methodology applied under paragraph (3) for single source drugs and biologicals furnished before , and using the methodology applied under paragraph (6) for single source drugs and biologicals furnished on or after , for all National Drug Codes assigned to such drug or biological product.April 1, 2008April 1, 2008

children.heading: Wholesale acquisition cost (WAC)
children.label: B Wholesale acquisition cost (WAC)
children.text: The wholesale acquisition cost (as defined in subsection (c)(6)(B)) using the methodology applied under paragraph (3) for single source drugs and biologicals furnished before , and using the methodology applied under paragraph (6) for single source drugs and biologicals furnished on or after , for all National Drug Codes assigned to such drug or biological product.April 1, 2008April 1, 2008

children.heading: Basis for payment amount
children.label: 5 Basis for payment amount
children.text: The payment amount shall be determined under this subsection based on information reported under subsection (f) and without regard to any special packaging, labeling, or identifiers on the dosage form or product or package.

children.heading: Use of volume-weighted average sales prices in calculation of average sales price
children.label: 6 Use of volume-weighted average sales prices in calculation of average sales price
I
children.text: the manufacturer’s average sales price (as defined in subsection (c)), determined by the Secretary without dividing such price by the total number of billing units for the National Drug Code for the billing and payment code; and

children.label: II
children.text: the total number of units specified under paragraph (2) sold; and

children.label: I
children.text: the total number of units specified under paragraph (2) sold; and

children.label: II
children.text: the total number of billing units for the National Drug Code for the billing and payment code.

children.heading: Billing unit defined
children.label: B Billing unit defined
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “billing unit” means the identifiable quantity associated with a billing and payment code, as established by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: the payment amount that would be determined for such drug or biological applying such subsection; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the payment amount that would have been determined for such drug or biological if such subsection were not applied; and

children.label: i
children.text: the payment amount that would be determined for such drug or biological taking into account the application of such subsection; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the payment amount that would have been determined for such drug or biological if such subsection were not applied.

children.label: i
children.text: the average sales price as determined using the methodology described under paragraph (6) applied to a biosimilar biological product for all National Drug Codes assigned to such product in the same manner as such paragraph is applied to drugs described in such paragraph; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 6 percent of the amount determined under paragraph (4) for the reference biological product (as defined in subsection (c)(6)(I)).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of a qualifying biosimilar biological product that is furnished during the applicable 5-year period for such product, the amount specified in this paragraph for such product with respect to such period is the sum determined under subparagraph (A), except that clause (ii) of such subparagraph shall be applied by substituting “8 percent” for “6 percent”.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of such a product for which payment was made under this paragraph as of , the 5-year period beginning on ; andSeptember 30, 2022October 1, 2022

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of such a product for which payment is first made under this paragraph during a calendar quarter during the period beginning , and ending , the 5-year period beginning on the first day of such calendar quarter during which such payment is first made.October 1, 2022December 31, 2027

children.heading: Qualifying biosimilar biological product defined
children.label: iii Qualifying biosimilar biological product defined
I
children.text: in the case of a product described in clause (ii)(I), the average sales price under paragraph (8)(A)(i) for a calendar quarter during the 5-year period described in such clause is not more than the average sales price under paragraph (4)(A) for such quarter for the reference biological product; and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a product described in clause (ii)(II), the average sales price under paragraph (8)(A)(i) for a calendar quarter during the 5-year period described in such clause is not more than the average sales price under paragraph (4)(A) for such quarter for the reference biological product.

children.label: A
children.text: the manufacturer’s sales to all purchasers (excluding sales exempted in paragraph (2)) in the United States for such drug or biological in the calendar quarter; divided by

children.label: B
children.text: the total number of such units of such drug or biological sold by the manufacturer in such quarter.

children.heading: Sales exempt from best price
children.label: A Sales exempt from best price
children.text: Sales exempt from the inclusion in the determination of “best price” under .section 1396r–8(c)(1)(C)(i) of this title

children.heading: Sales at nominal charge
children.label: B Sales at nominal charge
children.text: Such other sales as the Secretary identifies as sales to an entity that are merely nominal in amount (as applied for purposes of , except as the Secretary may otherwise provide).section 1396r–8(c)(1)(C)(ii)(III) of this title

children.heading: Sale price net of discounts
children.label: 3 Sale price net of discounts
children.text: In calculating the manufacturer’s average sales price under this subsection, such price shall include volume discounts, prompt pay discounts, cash discounts, free goods that are contingent on any purchase requirement, chargebacks, and rebates (other than rebates under subsection (i), , or ). For years after 2004, the Secretary may include in such price other price concessions, which may be based on recommendations of the Inspector General, that would result in a reduction of the cost to the purchaser.section 1396r–8 of this titlesection 1395w–114b of this title

children.heading: Payment methodology in cases where average sales price during first quarter of sales is unavailable
children.label: 4 Payment methodology in cases where average sales price during first quarter of sales is unavailable
I
children.text: the wholesale acquisition cost; or

children.label: II
children.text: the methodologies in effect under this part on , to determine payment amounts for drugs or biologicals; andNovember 1, 2003

children.label: I
children.text: at an amount not to exceed 103 percent of the wholesale acquisition cost; or

children.label: II
children.text: based on the methodologies in effect under this part on , to determine payment amounts for drugs or biologicals.November 1, 2003

children.heading: Limitation on payment amount for biosimilar biological products during initial period
children.label: B Limitation on payment amount for biosimilar biological products during initial period
i
children.text: The amount determined under clause (ii) of such subparagraph for the biosimilar biological product.

children.label: ii
children.text: The amount determined under subsection (b)(1)(B) for the reference biological product.

children.heading: In general on a quarterly basis
children.label: A In general on a quarterly basis
children.text: The manufacturer’s average sales price, for a drug or biological of a manufacturer, shall be calculated by such manufacturer under this subsection on a quarterly basis. In making such calculation insofar as there is a lag in the reporting of the information on rebates and chargebacks under paragraph (3) so that adequate data are not available on a timely basis, the manufacturer shall apply a methodology based on a 12-month rolling average for the manufacturer to estimate costs attributable to rebates and chargebacks. For years after 2004, the Secretary may establish a uniform methodology under this subparagraph to estimate and apply such costs.

children.heading: Updates in payment amounts
children.label: B Updates in payment amounts
children.text: The payment amounts under subsection (b) shall be updated by the Secretary on a quarterly basis and shall be applied based upon the manufacturer’s average sales price calculated for the most recent calendar quarter for which data is available.

children.heading: Use of contractors; implementation
children.label: C Use of contractors; implementation
children.text: The Secretary may contract with appropriate entities to calculate the payment amount under subsection (b). Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary may implement, by program instruction or otherwise, any of the provisions of this section.

children.heading: Manufacturer
children.label: A Manufacturer
children.text: The term “manufacturer” means, with respect to a drug or biological, the manufacturer (as defined in ), except that, for purposes of subsection (f)(2), the Secretary may, if the Secretary determines appropriate, exclude repackagers of a drug or biological from such term.section 1396r–8(k)(5) of this title

children.heading: Wholesale acquisition cost
children.label: B Wholesale acquisition cost
children.text: The term “wholesale acquisition cost” means, with respect to a drug or biological, the manufacturer’s list price for the drug or biological to wholesalers or direct purchasers in the United States, not including prompt pay or other discounts, rebates or reductions in price, for the most recent month for which the information is available, as reported in wholesale price guides or other publications of drug or biological pricing data.

children.label: I
children.text: are rated as therapeutically equivalent (under the Food and Drug Administration’s most recent publication of “Approved Drug Products with Therapeutic Equivalence Evaluations”),

children.label: II
children.text: except as provided in subparagraph (E), are pharmaceutically equivalent and bioequivalent, as determined under subparagraph (F) and as determined by the Food and Drug Administration, and

children.label: III
children.text: are sold or marketed in the United States during the quarter.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: With respect to single source drugs or biologicals that are within the same billing and payment code as of , the Secretary shall treat such single source drugs or biologicals as if the single source drugs or biologicals were multiple source drugs.October 1, 2003

children.heading: Single source drug or biological
children.label: D Single source drug or biological
i
children.text: a biological; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a drug which is not a multiple source drug and which is produced or distributed under a new drug application approved by the Food and Drug Administration, including a drug product marketed by any cross-licensed producers or distributors operating under the new drug application.

children.heading: Exception from pharmaceutical equivalence and bioequivalence requirement
children.label: E Exception from pharmaceutical equivalence and bioequivalence requirement
children.text: Subparagraph (C)(ii) shall not apply if the Food and Drug Administration changes by regulation the requirement that, for purposes of the publication described in subparagraph (C)(i), in order for drug products to be rated as therapeutically equivalent, they must be pharmaceutically equivalent and bioequivalent, as defined in subparagraph (F).

children.heading: Determination of pharmaceutical equivalence and bioequivalence
children.label: F Determination of pharmaceutical equivalence and bioequivalence
i
children.text: drug products are pharmaceutically equivalent if the products contain identical amounts of the same active drug ingredient in the same dosage form and meet compendial or other applicable standards of strength, quality, purity, and identity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: drugs are bioequivalent if they do not present a known or potential bioequivalence problem, or, if they do present such a problem, they are shown to meet an appropriate standard of bioequivalence.

children.heading: Inclusion of vaccines
children.label: G Inclusion of vaccines
children.text: In applying provisions of under this section, “other than a vaccine” is deemed deleted from .section 1396r–8 of this titlesection 1396r–8(k)(2)(B) of this title

children.heading: Biosimilar biological product
children.label: H Biosimilar biological product
children.text: The term “biosimilar biological product” means a biological product approved under an abbreviated application for a license of a biological product that relies in part on data or information in an application for another biological product licensed under .section 262 of this title

children.heading: Reference biological product
children.label: I Reference biological product
children.text: The term “reference biological product” means the biological product licensed under such that is referred to in the application described in subparagraph (H) of the biosimilar biological product.section 262 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services shall conduct studies, which may include surveys, to determine the widely available market prices of drugs and biologicals to which this section applies, as the Inspector General, in consultation with the Secretary, determines to be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: the widely available market price for such drugs and biologicals (if any); and

children.label: B
children.text: the average manufacturer price (as determined under ) for such drugs and biologicals.section 1396r–8(k)(1) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may disregard the average sales price for a drug or biological that exceeds the widely available market price or the average manufacturer price for such drug or biological by the applicable threshold percentage (as defined in subparagraph (B)).

children.label: i
children.text: in 2005, in the case of an average sales price for a drug or biological that exceeds widely available market price or the average manufacturer price, 5 percent; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in 2006 and subsequent years, the percentage applied under this subparagraph subject to such adjustment as the Secretary may specify for the widely available market price or the average manufacturer price, or both.

children.heading: Authority to adjust average sales price
children.label: C Authority to adjust average sales price
i
children.text: the widely available market price for the drug or biological (if any); or

children.label: ii
children.text: 103 percent of the average manufacturer price (as determined under ) for the drug or biological.section 1396r–8(k)(1) of this title

children.heading: Misrepresentation
children.label: A Misrepresentation
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a manufacturer has made a misrepresentation in the reporting of the manufacturer’s average sales price for a drug or biological, the Secretary may apply a civil money penalty in an amount of up to $10,000 for each such price misrepresentation and for each day in which such price misrepresentation was applied.

children.heading: Failure to provide timely information
children.label: B Failure to provide timely information
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a manufacturer described in subsection (f)(2) has failed to report on information described in with respect to a drug or biological in accordance with such subsection, the Secretary shall apply a civil money penalty in an amount of $10,000 for each day the manufacturer has failed to report such information and such amount shall be paid to the Treasury.section 1396r–8(b)(3)(A)(iii) of this title

children.heading: False information
children.label: C False information
children.text: Any manufacturer required to submit information under subsection (f)(2) that knowingly provides false information is subject to a civil money penalty in an amount not to exceed $100,000 for each item of false information. Such civil money penalties are in addition to other penalties as may be prescribed by law.

children.label: i
children.text: each reference to “under this subparagraph and subsection (c)(4)(B)(ii)(III)” were a reference to “under this subparagraph, subsection (c)(4)(B)(ii)(III), and subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of ”; andsection 1395w–3a(d)(4) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the reference to “activities related to the oversight and enforcement of this section and agreements under this section” were a reference to “activities related to the oversight and enforcement of this section and under subsection (f)(2) of and subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of this sub and, if applicable, agreements under this section”.section 1395w–3a of this titlesection 1395w–3a(d)(4) of this title

children.heading: Procedures
children.label: E Procedures
children.text: The provisions of (other than subsections (a) and (b)) shall apply to civil money penalties under subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) in the same manner as they apply to a penalty or proceeding under .section 1320a–7a of this titlesection 1320a–7a(a) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In this subsection, the term “widely available market price” means the price that a prudent physician or supplier would pay for the drug or biological. In determining such price, the Inspector General shall take into account the discounts, rebates, and other price concessions routinely made available to such prudent physicians or suppliers for such drugs or biologicals.

children.label: i
children.text: Manufacturers.

children.label: ii
children.text: Wholesalers.

children.label: iii
children.text: Distributors.

children.label: iv
children.text: Physician supply houses.

children.label: v
children.text: Specialty pharmacies.

children.label: vi
children.text: Group purchasing arrangements.

children.label: vii
children.text: Surveys of physicians.

children.label: viii
children.text: Surveys of suppliers.

children.label: ix
children.text: Information on such market prices from insurers.

children.label: x
children.text: Information on such market prices from private health plans.

children.heading: Authority to use alternative payment in response to public health emergency
children.label: e Authority to use alternative payment in response to public health emergency
children.text: In the case of a public health emergency under in which there is a documented inability to access drugs and biologicals, and a concomitant increase in the price, of a drug or biological which is not reflected in the manufacturer’s average sales price for one or more quarters, the Secretary may use the wholesale acquisition cost (or other reasonable measure of drug or biological price) instead of the manufacturer’s average sales price for such quarters and for subsequent quarters until the price and availability of the drug or biological has stabilized and is substantially reflected in the applicable manufacturer’s average sales price.section 247d of this title1So in original. The comma probably should not appear.1

children.heading: Quarterly report on average sales price
children.label: f Quarterly report on average sales price

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For requirements for reporting the manufacturer’s average sales price (and, if required to make payment, the manufacturer’s wholesale acquisition cost) for the drug or biological under this section, see .section 1396r–8(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: Manufacturers without a rebate agreement under subchapter xix
children.label: 2 Manufacturers without a rebate agreement under subchapter xix

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the manufacturer of a drug or biological described in subparagraph (C), (E), or (G) of section 1395u()(1) of this title or in that is payable under this part has not entered into and does not have in effect a rebate agreement described in subsection (b) of , for calendar quarters beginning on , such manufacturer shall report to the Secretary the information described in subsection (b)(3)(A)(iii) of such with respect to such drug or biological in a time and manner specified by the Secretary. For purposes of applying this paragraph, a drug or biological described in the previous sentence includes items, services, supplies, and products that are payable under this part as a drug or biological.osection 1395rr(b)(14)(B) of this titlesection 1396r–8 of this titlesection 1396r–8 of this titleJanuary 1, 2022

children.heading: Audit
children.label: B Audit
children.text: Information reported under subparagraph (A) is subject to audit by the Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: Verification
children.label: C Verification
children.text: The Secretary may survey wholesalers and manufacturers that directly distribute drugs or biologicals described in subparagraph (A), when necessary, to verify manufacturer prices and manufacturer’s average sales prices (including wholesale acquisition cost) if required to make payment reported under subparagraph (A). The Secretary may impose a civil monetary penalty in an amount not to exceed $100,000 on a wholesaler, manufacturer, or direct seller, if the wholesaler, manufacturer, or direct seller of such a drug or biological refuses a request for information about charges or prices by the Secretary in connection with a survey under this subparagraph or knowingly provides false information. The provisions of (other than subsections (a) (with respect to amounts of penalties or additional assessments) and (b)) shall apply to a civil money penalty under this subparagraph in the same manner as such provisions apply to a penalty or proceeding under .section 1320a–7a of this titlesection 1320a–7a(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: as the Secretary determines to be necessary to carry out this section (including the determination and implementation of the payment amount), or to carry out ;section 1320a–7b of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: to permit the Comptroller General of the United States to review the information provided;

children.label: iii
children.text: to permit the Director of the Congressional Budget Office to review the information provided;

children.label: iv
children.text: to permit the Medicare Payment Advisory Commission to review the information provided; and

children.label: v
children.text: to permit the Medicaid and CHIP Payment and Access Commission to review the information provided.

children.heading: Payment adjustment for certain drugs for which there is a self-administered NDC
children.label: g Payment adjustment for certain drugs for which there is a self-administered NDC

children.heading: OIG studies
children.label: 1 OIG studies
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services shall conduct periodic studies to identify National Drug Codes for drug or biological products that are self-administered for which payment may not be made under this part because such products are not covered pursuant to and which the Inspector General determines (based on the same or similar methodologies to the methodologies used in the final recommendation followup report of the Inspector General described in paragraph (3) or in the November 2017 final report of the Inspector General entitled “Excluding Noncovered Versions When Setting Payment for Two Part B Drugs Would Have Resulted in Lower Drug Costs for Medicare and its Beneficiaries”) should be excluded from the determination of the payment amount under this section.section 1395x(s)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of payment that would be determined under this section for such billing and payment code if such National Drug Code for such product so identified under paragraph (1) were excluded from such determination; or

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of payment otherwise determined under this section for such billing and payment code without application of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of payment that would be determined under this section for such billing and payment code if such National Drug Code for such drug or biological products so identified were excluded from such determination; or

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of payment otherwise determined under this section for such billing and payment code without application of this subsection.

children.heading: Refund for certain discarded single-dose container or single-use package drugs
children.label: h Refund for certain discarded single-dose container or single-use package drugs
i
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (C), information on the total number of units of the billing and payment code of such drug, if any, that were discarded during such quarter, as determined using a mechanism such as the JW modifier used as of (or any such successor modifier that includes such data as determined appropriate by the Secretary).November 15, 2021

children.label: ii
children.text: The refund amount that the manufacturer is liable for pursuant to paragraph (3).

children.heading: Determination of discarded amounts
children.label: B Determination of discarded amounts
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)(i), with respect to a refundable single-dose container or single-use package drug furnished during a quarter, the amount of such drug that was discarded shall be determined based on the amount of such drug that was unused and discarded for each drug on the date of service.

children.heading: Exclusion of units of packaged drugs
children.label: C Exclusion of units of packaged drugs
children.text: The total number of units of the billing and payment code of a refundable single-dose container or single-use package drug of a manufacturer furnished during a calendar quarter for purposes of subparagraph (A)(i), and the determination of the estimated total allowed charges for the drug in the quarter for purposes of paragraph (3)(A)(ii), shall not include such units that are packaged into the payment amount for an item or service and are not separately payable.

children.heading: Manufacturer requirement
children.label: 2 Manufacturer requirement
children.text: For each calendar quarter beginning on or after , the manufacturer of a refundable single-dose container or single-use package drug shall, for such drug, provide to the Secretary a refund that is equal to the amount specified in paragraph (3) for such drug for such quarter.January 1, 2023

children.label: I
children.text: the total number of units of the billing and payment code for such drug that were discarded during such quarter (as determined under paragraph (1)); and

children.label: aa
children.text: in the case of a refundable single-dose container or single-use package drug that is a single source drug or biological, the amount of payment determined for such drug or biological under subsection (b)(1)(B) for such quarter; or

children.label: bb
children.text: in the case of a refundable single-dose container or single-use package drug that is a biosimilar biological product, the amount of payment determined for such product under subsection (b)(1)(C) for such quarter; exceeds

children.label: ii
children.text: an amount equal to the applicable percentage (as defined in subparagraph (B)) of the estimated total allowed charges for such drug under this part during the quarter.

children.label: I
children.text: subject to subclause (II), 10 percent; and

children.label: II
children.text: if applicable, in the case of a refundable single-dose container or single-use package drug described in clause (ii), a percentage specified by the Secretary pursuant to such clause.

children.heading: Treatment of drugs that have unique circumstances
children.label: ii Treatment of drugs that have unique circumstances
children.text: In the case of a refundable single-dose container or single-use package drug that has unique circumstances involving similar loss of product as that described in paragraph (8)(B)(ii), the Secretary, through notice and comment rulemaking, may increase the applicable percentage otherwise applicable under clause (i)(I) as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Frequency
children.label: 4 Frequency
children.text: Amounts required to be refunded pursuant to paragraph (2) shall be paid in regular intervals (as determined appropriate by the Secretary).

children.heading: Refund deposits
children.label: 5 Refund deposits
children.text: Amounts paid as refunds pursuant to paragraph (2) shall be deposited into the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund established under .section 1395t of this title

children.heading: Manufacturer audits
children.label: i Manufacturer audits
children.text: Each manufacturer of a refundable single-dose container or single-use package drug that is required to provide a refund under this subsection shall be subject to periodic audit with respect to such drug and such refunds by the Secretary.

children.heading: Provider audits
children.label: ii Provider audits
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct periodic audits of claims submitted under this part with respect to refundable single-dose container or single-use package drugs in accordance with the authority under section 1395(e) of this title to ensure compliance with the requirements applicable under this subsection.l

children.label: I
children.text: the amount that the manufacturer would have paid under such paragraph with respect to such drug for such quarter; and

children.label: II
children.text: 25 percent of such amount.

children.heading: Application
children.label: ii Application
children.text: The provisions of (other than subsections (a) and (b)) shall apply to a civil money penalty under this subparagraph in the same manner as such provisions apply to a penalty or proceeding under .section 1320a–7a of this titlesection 1320a–7a(a) of this title

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 7 Implementation
children.text: The Secretary shall implement this subsection through notice and comment rulemaking.

children.heading: Definition of refundable single-dose container or single-use package drug
children.label: 8 Definition of refundable single-dose container or single-use package drug

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), in this subsection, the term “refundable single-dose container or single-use package drug” means a single source drug or biological (as defined in subsection (c)(6)(D)) or a biosimilar biological product (as defined in subsection (c)(6)(H)) for which payment is made under this part and that is furnished from a single-dose container or single-use package.

children.label: i
children.text: a drug or biological that is either a radiopharmaceutical or an imaging agent;

children.label: ii
children.text: a drug or biological approved by the Food and Drug Administration for which dosage and administration instructions included in the labeling require filtration during the drug preparation process, prior to dilution and administration, and require that any unused portion of such drug after the filtration process be discarded after the completion of such filtration process; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a drug or biological approved by the Food and Drug Administration on or after , and with respect to which payment has been made under this part for fewer than 18 months.November 15, 2021

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: 9 Report to Congress
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , the Office of the Inspector General, after consultation with the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services and the Food and Drug Administration, shall submit to the Committee on Finance of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives, a report on any impact this section is reported to have on the licensure, market entry, market retention, or marketing of biosimilar biological products. Such report shall be updated periodically at the direction of the Committee on Finance of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives.November 15, 2021

children.heading: Rebate by manufacturers for single source drugs and biologicals with prices increasing faster than inflation
children.label: i Rebate by manufacturers for single source drugs and biologicals with prices increasing faster than inflation
i
children.text: Information on the total number of units of the billing and payment code described in subparagraph (A)(i) of paragraph (3) with respect to such drug and calendar quarter.

children.label: ii
children.text: Information on the amount (if any) of the excess average sales price increase described in subparagraph (A)(ii) of such paragraph for such drug and calendar quarter.

children.label: iii
children.text: The rebate amount specified under such paragraph for such part B rebatable drug and calendar quarter.

children.heading: Manufacturer requirement
children.label: B Manufacturer requirement
children.text: For each calendar quarter beginning on or after , the manufacturer of a part B rebatable drug shall, for such drug, not later than 30 days after the date of receipt from the Secretary of the information described in subparagraph (A) for such calendar quarter, provide to the Secretary a rebate that is equal to the amount specified in paragraph (3) for such drug for such calendar quarter.January 1, 2023

children.heading: Transition rule for reporting
children.label: C Transition rule for reporting
children.text: The Secretary may, for each part B rebatable drug, delay the timeframe for reporting the information described in subparagraph (A) for calendar quarters beginning in 2023 and 2024 until not later than .September 30, 2025

children.label: i
children.text: if, as determined by the Secretary, the average total allowed charges for such drug or biological under this part for a year per individual that uses such a drug or biological are less than, subject to subparagraph (B), $100; or

children.label: ii
children.text: that is a vaccine described in subparagraph (A) or (B) of .section 1395x(s)(10) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: for 2024, shall be the dollar amount specified under such subparagraph for 2023, increased by the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (United States city average) for the 12-month period ending with June of the previous year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for a subsequent year, shall be the dollar amount specified in this clause (or clause (i)) for the previous year (without application of subparagraph (C)), increased by the percentage increase in the consumer price index for all urban consumers (United States city average) for the 12-month period ending with June of the previous year.

children.heading: Rounding
children.label: C Rounding
children.text: Any dollar amount determined under subparagraph (B) that is not a multiple of $10 shall be rounded to the nearest multiple of $10.

children.label: i
children.text: the total number of units determined under subparagraph (B) for the billing and payment code of such drug; and

children.label: aa
children.text: in the case of a part B rebatable drug described in paragraph (1)(B) of subsection (b), 106 percent of the amount determined under paragraph (4) of such section for such drug during the calendar quarter; or2So in original. Probably should be “subsection”.2

children.label: bb
children.text: in the case of a part B rebatable drug described in paragraph (1)(C) of such subsection, the payment amount under such paragraph for such drug during the calendar quarter; exceeds

children.label: II
children.text: the inflation-adjusted payment amount determined under subparagraph (C) for such part B rebatable drug during the calendar quarter.

children.label: i
children.text: the number of units for the billing and payment code of such drug furnished during such calendar quarter, minus

children.label: I
children.text: with respect to which the manufacturer provides a discount under the program under or a rebate under ; orsection 256b of this titlesection 1396r–8 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: that are packaged into the payment amount for an item or service and are not separately payable.

children.heading: Determination of inflation-adjusted payment amount
children.label: C Determination of inflation-adjusted payment amount
i
children.text: the payment amount for the billing and payment code for such drug in the payment amount benchmark quarter (as defined in subparagraph (D)); increased by

children.label: ii
children.text: the percentage by which the rebate period CPI–U (as defined in subparagraph (F)) for the calendar quarter exceeds the benchmark period CPI–U (as defined in subparagraph (E)).

children.heading: Payment amount benchmark quarter
children.label: D Payment amount benchmark quarter
children.text: The term “payment amount benchmark quarter” means the calendar quarter beginning .July 1, 2021

children.heading: Benchmark period CPI–U
children.label: E Benchmark period CPI–U
children.text: The term “benchmark period CPI–U” means the consumer price index for all urban consumers (United States city average) for January 2021.

children.heading: Rebate period CPI–U
children.label: F Rebate period CPI–U
children.text: The term “rebate period CPI–U” means, with respect to a calendar quarter described in subparagraph (C), the greater of the benchmark period CPI–U and the consumer price index for all urban consumers (United States city average) for the first month of the calendar quarter that is two calendar quarters prior to such described calendar quarter.

children.heading: Reduction or waiver for shortages and severe supply chain disruptions
children.label: G Reduction or waiver for shortages and severe supply chain disruptions
i
children.text: in the case of a part B rebatable drug that is described as currently in shortage on the shortage list in effect under at any point during the calendar quarter; orsection 356e of title 21

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a biosimilar biological product, when the Secretary determines there is a severe supply chain disruption during the calendar quarter, such as that caused by a natural disaster or other unique or unexpected event.

children.heading: Special treatment of certain drugs and exemption
children.label: 4 Special treatment of certain drugs and exemption

children.heading: Subsequently approved drugs
children.label: A Subsequently approved drugs
children.text: In the case of a part B rebatable drug first approved or licensed by the Food and Drug Administration after , clause (i) of paragraph (3)(C) shall be applied as if the term “payment amount benchmark quarter” were defined under paragraph (3)(D) as the third full calendar quarter after the day on which the drug was first marketed and clause (ii) of paragraph (3)(C) shall be applied as if the term “benchmark period CPI–U” were defined under paragraph (3)(E) as if the reference to “January 2021” under such paragraph were a reference to “the first month of the first full calendar quarter after the day on which the drug was first marketed”.December 1, 2020

children.heading: Timeline for provision of rebates for subsequently approved drugs
children.label: B Timeline for provision of rebates for subsequently approved drugs
children.text: In the case of a part B rebatable drug first approved or licensed by the Food and Drug Administration after , paragraph (1)(B) shall be applied as if the reference to “” under such paragraph were a reference to “the later of the 6th full calendar quarter after the day on which the drug was first marketed or ”.December 1, 2020January 1, 2023January 1, 2023

children.heading: Selected drugs
children.label: C Selected drugs
children.text: In the case of a part B rebatable drug that is a selected drug (as defined in ) with respect to a price applicability period (as defined in ), in the case such drug is no longer considered to be a selected drug under , for each applicable period (as defined under subsection (g)(7)) beginning after the price applicability period with respect to such drug, clause (i) of paragraph (3)(C) shall be applied as if the term “payment amount benchmark quarter” were defined under paragraph (3)(D) as the calendar quarter beginning January 1 of the last year during such price applicability period with respect to such selected drug and clause (ii) of paragraph (3)(C) shall be applied as if the term “benchmark period CPI–U” were defined under paragraph (3)(E) as if the reference to “January 2021” under such paragraph were a reference to “the July of the year preceding such last year”.section 1320f–1(c) of this titlesection 1320f(b)(2) of this titlesection 1320f–1(c) of this title3See References in Text note below.3

children.label: A
children.text: in computing the amount of any coinsurance applicable under this part to an individual to whom such drug is furnished, the computation of such coinsurance shall be equal to 20 percent of the inflation-adjusted payment amount determined under paragraph (3)(C) for such part B rebatable drug; and

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of such coinsurance for such calendar quarter, as computed under subparagraph (A), shall be applied as a percent, as determined by the Secretary, to the payment amount that would otherwise apply under subparagraphs (B) or (C) of subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Rebate deposits
children.label: 6 Rebate deposits
children.text: Amounts paid as rebates under paragraph (1)(B) shall be deposited into the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund established under .section 1395t of this title

children.heading: Civil money penalty
children.label: 7 Civil money penalty
children.text: If a manufacturer of a part B rebatable drug has failed to comply with the requirements under paragraph (1)(B) for such drug for a calendar quarter, the manufacturer shall be subject to, in accordance with a process established by the Secretary pursuant to regulations, a civil money penalty in an amount equal to at least 125 percent of the amount specified in paragraph (3) for such drug for such calendar quarter. The provisions of (other than subsections (a) (with respect to amounts of penalties or additional assessments) and (b)) shall apply to a civil money penalty under this paragraph in the same manner as such provisions apply to a penalty or proceeding under .section 1320a–7a of this titlesection 1320a–7a(a) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on administrative or judicial review
children.label: 8 Limitation on administrative or judicial review
A
children.text: The determination of units under this subsection.

children.label: B
children.text: The determination of whether a drug is a part B rebatable drug under this subsection.

children.label: C
children.text: The calculation of the rebate amount under this subsection.

children.label: D
children.text: The computation of coinsurance under paragraph (5) of this subsection.

children.label: E
children.text: The computation of amounts paid under section 1395(a)(1)(EE) of this title.l
determinations of payment amounts under this section, including the assignment of National Drug Codes to billing and payment codes;
the identification of units (and package size) under subsection (b)(2);
the method to allocate rebates, chargebacks, and other price concessions to a quarter if specified by the Secretary;
the manufacturer’s average sales price when it is used for the determination of a payment amount under this section; and
the disclosure of the average manufacturer price by reason of an adjustment under subsection (d)(3)(C) or (e).
